title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration
description: Rendering consumes random draws, replay stores key state without timing, floats are compared as formatted text, settings overwrite atomically unsafe, and corrupted saves silently reset. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible replay and versioned local state through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.
